question: Which of the following is the best description of an environmental pollutant?

options.A: Any substance that is colourful in nature
options.B: Any substance present in the environment in a quantity large enough to cause harm
options.C: Any material that dissolves in water
options.D: Any gas found in the atmosphere

answer_text: Any substance present in the environment in a quantity large enough to cause harm

explanation.question_restate: Which of the following is the best description of an environmental pollutant?
explanation.principle: A pollutant is not defined merely by being present. It becomes a pollutant when its amount or effect becomes harmful to living things, materials, or the environment.
steps: The environment normally contains many natural substances such as water vapour, carbon dioxide and mineral salts.
Those substances are not automatically pollutants just because they exist.
A substance is called a pollutant when it is introduced into the environment, or builds up there, at a level that causes damage.
The damage may be to human health, crops, animals, buildings, water bodies or soil.
For example, smoke, sewage, oil spills and poisonous gases become pollutants because of their harmful effects.
So the key idea in examination questions is harm, not simply presence.
explanation.final_answer: B. Any substance present in the environment in a quantity large enough to cause harm
explanation.simple_explanation: A pollutant is a harmful substance, or a normally harmless substance present in harmful quantity. That makes option B correct.

question: A major difference between pollution and contamination is that pollution

options.B: is always natural while contamination is artificial
options.C: produces harmful effects on the environment

answer_text: produces harmful effects on the environment

explanation.question_restate: A major difference between pollution and contamination is that pollution
explanation.principle: Many exam questions test the idea that contamination means unwanted presence, while pollution emphasizes harmful impact.
steps: Contamination means a place or substance has been made impure by the presence of an unwanted material.
However, that material may not yet have caused serious damage.
Pollution goes further because it means the contamination is harmful.
The harm may affect breathing, drinking water, crop yield, visibility, aquatic life or soil fertility.
Therefore, whenever JAMB asks for the best distinguishing feature of pollution, look for damage or harmful effect.
Option C expresses that idea clearly.
explanation.final_answer: C. produces harmful effects on the environment
explanation.simple_explanation: Pollution is harmful contamination. The important word is harm, so option C is correct.

question: Which of the following is mainly a domestic source of environmental pollution?

explanation.question_restate: Which of the following is mainly a domestic source of environmental pollution?
explanation.principle: Domestic pollution comes mainly from homes and human settlements, unlike industrial pollution which comes from factories and heavy industry.
steps: Domestic sources are pollutants produced in daily living around homes, schools, markets and neighborhoods.
Examples include refuse heaps, sewage, detergents in wastewater, smoke from cooking and poor sanitation.
Industrial pollution comes from factories, mines, refineries and power stations.
Blast furnaces, refinery stacks and quarry dust belong mainly to industry.
Refuse dumping and sewage discharge are typical domestic sources and are common causes of offensive odour, water pollution and disease spread.
Hence option B is the domestic source.
explanation.final_answer: B. Refuse dumping and sewage discharge
explanation.simple_explanation: Waste from homes, especially refuse and sewage, is a domestic source of pollution. So option B is correct.

question: One serious effect common to air, water and soil pollution is

explanation.question_restate: One serious effect common to air, water and soil pollution is
explanation.principle: Although the form of pollution differs, the broad effect is damage to life and environmental quality.
steps: Air pollution can injure lungs, eyes, leaves and animals.
Water pollution can kill fish, spread diseases and make water unsafe for domestic use.
Soil pollution can reduce fertility, poison crops and affect soil organisms.
Because all three affect organisms directly or indirectly, the broadest correct effect is harm to living things.
Options A, B and D describe beneficial results, which are not the normal outcomes of pollution.
Therefore the best answer is option C.
explanation.final_answer: C. harm to living organisms
explanation.simple_explanation: No matter the type, pollution generally damages life. That is why option C is correct.

question: The accumulation of non-biodegradable materials in the tissues of organisms along a food chain is known as

explanation.question_restate: The accumulation of non-biodegradable materials in the tissues of organisms along a food chain is known as
explanation.principle: Some pollutants do not break down easily. They can remain in living tissues and build up over time, especially through feeding relationships.
steps: Non-biodegradable pollutants are substances that microorganisms cannot easily decompose.
Examples include many plastics, some pesticides and heavy metals.
When such materials enter an ecosystem, they may be taken up by small organisms.
Larger organisms then feed on the smaller ones and receive even more of the pollutant.
As the pollutant concentration builds up in tissues, we describe the process as bioaccumulation.
Students sometimes confuse this with simple settling or separation methods such as sedimentation and distillation, but those are not biological build-up processes.
explanation.final_answer: B. bioaccumulation
explanation.simple_explanation: When harmful substances build up in body tissues and become more concentrated in organisms, the process is called bioaccumulation. So option B is correct.

question: Which statement about biodegradable pollutants is correct?

options.A: They can be broken down by microorganisms
options.B: They remain unchanged for centuries in all cases
options.C: They are always harmless to health
options.D: They are found only in air

answer_text: They can be broken down by microorganisms

explanation.question_restate: Which statement about biodegradable pollutants is correct?
explanation.principle: Biodegradable materials are those that bacteria and other decomposers can act upon.
steps: Biodegradable pollutants include many organic wastes such as sewage, paper, vegetable matter and some food wastes.
Microorganisms such as bacteria and fungi decompose them into simpler substances.
That is why the word 'bio' refers to living organisms and 'degradable' refers to being broken down.
However, biodegradable does not mean harmless. If present in excess, such waste can still pollute water and consume dissolved oxygen.
So option C is wrong.
The defining property is that they can be decomposed biologically.
explanation.final_answer: A. They can be broken down by microorganisms
explanation.simple_explanation: Biodegradable pollutants are broken down by living decomposers. Therefore option A is correct.

question: Which of the following is most likely to be a non-biodegradable pollutant?

explanation.question_restate: Which of the following is most likely to be a non-biodegradable pollutant?
explanation.principle: Non-biodegradable pollutants resist natural decomposition and therefore persist in the environment for a long time.
steps: Cow dung, sewage and vegetable peels are organic wastes.
Microorganisms can act on them and eventually break them down, although they may cause pollution before decomposition is complete.
Plastics are different because many common plastic materials do not rot quickly.
They can stay in gutters, rivers, fields and dumpsites for many years.
Because they persist, they are typical examples of non-biodegradable pollutants.
Therefore option C is the best answer.
explanation.final_answer: C. Plastic packaging
explanation.simple_explanation: Plastics do not decay easily, so they are a common non-biodegradable pollutant. Option C is correct.

question: The offensive smell noticed near a refuse dump is mainly due to

explanation.question_restate: The offensive smell noticed near a refuse dump is mainly due to
explanation.principle: Many bad odours around waste sites arise when microorganisms break down organic matter and release gases.
steps: Refuse dumps often contain food remains, leaves, faecal matter and other organic waste.
Bacteria and fungi act on these substances.
During decomposition, foul-smelling gases such as H₂S and ammonia may be released.
The odour is therefore linked mainly to biological decay, not to simple evaporation or condensation.
Melting of glass and condensation of oxygen are unrelated to the typical smell of decaying waste.
Hence option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. decomposition of waste by microorganisms
explanation.simple_explanation: Waste smells because microorganisms decompose organic matter and release bad-smelling gases. So option A is correct.

question: Which activity is most likely to reduce environmental pollution in a community?

explanation.question_restate: Which activity is most likely to reduce environmental pollution in a community?
explanation.principle: Pollution control aims at reducing the harmful effect of wastes before they enter the environment.
steps: Open burning releases smoke, soot and toxic gases into the air.
Improper disposal of used engine oil contaminates soil and water.
Dumping sewage into streams pollutes water and spreads disease.
Treating waste before discharge removes or reduces harmful substances.
For example, sewage treatment reduces microorganisms and organic load before release.
Therefore treatment of waste is a pollution-control measure and the best answer.
explanation.final_answer: C. Treatment of waste before discharge
explanation.simple_explanation: The correct preventive action is to treat waste before releasing it. That makes option C correct.

question: Which of the following pairs correctly matches a pollutant with a likely effect?

options.A: CO — improved oxygen transport in blood

explanation.question_restate: Which of the following pairs correctly matches a pollutant with a likely effect?
explanation.principle: Good exam technique involves matching each pollutant to its known effect accurately.
steps: CO is dangerous because it reduces oxygen transport in blood; it does not improve it.
SO₂ dissolves in atmospheric moisture and contributes to acidic precipitation.
Dust lowers visibility and may irritate the respiratory tract, so option C is false.
Sewage pollutes water instead of purifying it.
Thus the only correctly matched pair is SO₂ with acid rain formation.
This makes option B correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. SO₂ — acid rain formation
explanation.simple_explanation: SO₂ contributes to acid rain, so option B is the only correct pairing.

question: Which gas is especially dangerous because it combines strongly with haemoglobin in blood?

explanation.question_restate: Which gas is especially dangerous because it combines strongly with haemoglobin in blood?
explanation.principle: Carbon monoxide is a poisonous gas because it interferes directly with oxygen transport in the body.
steps: Haemoglobin in red blood cells normally carries oxygen from the lungs to body tissues.
Carbon monoxide, CO, competes with oxygen for the same binding sites on haemoglobin.
It combines much more strongly with haemoglobin than oxygen does.
As a result, less oxygen is carried round the body.
This can cause headache, weakness, unconsciousness and even death.
That is why incomplete combustion in generators, charcoal fires and vehicle exhausts is very dangerous.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. CO
explanation.simple_explanation: CO is dangerous because it ties up haemoglobin and prevents enough oxygen from being carried in the blood. So option B is correct.

question: Carbon monoxide is mainly produced during

explanation.question_restate: Carbon monoxide is mainly produced during
explanation.principle: CO is formed when there is not enough oxygen for carbon to burn completely to CO₂.
steps: When a fuel containing carbon burns in plenty of oxygen, the main carbon oxide formed is CO₂.
When the oxygen supply is poor, combustion is incomplete.
Under incomplete combustion, some carbon is converted to CO instead of CO₂.
This often occurs in faulty engines, indoor charcoal stoves and poorly ventilated generator areas.
Because of this, incomplete combustion is the major source of carbon monoxide.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. incomplete combustion of carbon compounds
explanation.simple_explanation: Too little oxygen during burning produces CO. Hence option B is correct.

question: A common symptom of exposure to hydrogen sulphide, H₂S, at low concentration is

explanation.question_restate: A common symptom of exposure to hydrogen sulphide, H₂S, at low concentration is
explanation.principle: Hydrogen sulphide has a characteristic offensive smell often used to identify it in simple chemistry questions.
steps: H₂S is a poisonous gas produced during decay of sulphur-containing organic matter and in some industrial activities.
One of its most familiar features is its rotten-egg smell.
This odour often helps people notice its presence at low concentration.
However, at high concentration it can still be very dangerous.
The other options do not describe the common identifying smell of H₂S.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. rotten-egg smell
explanation.simple_explanation: H₂S is recognized by its rotten-egg odour, so option B is correct.

question: The gas mainly responsible for the brownish appearance of photochemical smog is

explanation.question_restate: The gas mainly responsible for the brownish appearance of photochemical smog is
explanation.principle: Nitrogen dioxide is a reddish-brown gas and an important component of urban smog.
steps: Oxides of nitrogen are produced mainly at high temperatures in vehicle engines and industrial furnaces.
Among them, NO₂ is a reddish-brown gas.
This colour contributes to the brown haze sometimes seen over busy cities.
CO is colourless, H₂S is colourless though foul-smelling, and H₂ is also colourless.
Because of both colour and role in smog, NO₂ is the correct answer.
Therefore option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. NO₂
explanation.simple_explanation: NO₂ is the brown gas associated with smog. So option A is correct.

question: One major environmental effect of sulphur(IV) oxide, SO₂, is that it

options.A: destroys chlorophyll directly in all plants only
options.B: causes acid rain after reacting with atmospheric moisture

answer_text: causes acid rain after reacting with atmospheric moisture

explanation.question_restate: One major environmental effect of sulphur(IV) oxide, SO₂, is that it
explanation.principle: SO₂ is an acidic oxide and can lead to acidic precipitation.
steps: SO₂ enters the atmosphere mainly from burning sulphur-containing fuels and some industrial processes.
In the atmosphere, it can dissolve in water droplets.
Further reactions may produce acids, which fall as acid rain.
Acid rain can damage roofs, corrode metals, acidify lakes and harm vegetation.
SO₂ does not produce hard water, and it does not increase the ozone layer.
So option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. causes acid rain after reacting with atmospheric moisture
explanation.simple_explanation: SO₂ contributes to acid rain, so option B is correct.

question: Which of the following is a common source of oxides of nitrogen in the atmosphere?

options.D: Evaporation of petrol only at room temperature

explanation.question_restate: Which of the following is a common source of oxides of nitrogen in the atmosphere?
explanation.principle: Oxides of nitrogen form when nitrogen and oxygen in air react at very high temperatures.
steps: Air contains a lot of nitrogen and oxygen.
Under ordinary conditions they do not combine easily.
But inside vehicle engines and some industrial furnaces, temperatures become very high.
At such temperatures, nitrogen and oxygen can react to form oxides of nitrogen such as NO and NO₂.
This is why heavy traffic contributes strongly to urban air pollution.
Hence option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. High-temperature combustion in car engines
explanation.simple_explanation: Vehicle engines provide the high temperature needed for oxides of nitrogen to form. Therefore option A is correct.

question: Which pollutant is chiefly associated with depletion of the ozone layer?

explanation.question_restate: Which pollutant is chiefly associated with depletion of the ozone layer?
explanation.principle: This is a standard environmental chemistry fact often tested in objective questions.
steps: Chlorofluorocarbons, often abbreviated as CFCs, were formerly used in refrigerators, aerosol propellants and some foam products.
In the upper atmosphere, they can release chlorine-containing species.
These species take part in reactions that destroy ozone molecules.
The ozone layer is important because it absorbs harmful ultraviolet radiation from the Sun.
CO and dust cause other problems, but they are not the main substances linked to ozone depletion.
Therefore option C is correct.
explanation.final_answer: C. chlorofluorocarbons
explanation.simple_explanation: CFCs are the classic pollutants linked with ozone layer depletion. So option C is correct.

question: Dust particles in air are most likely to cause

options.C: increase in dissolved oxygen in rivers

explanation.question_restate: Dust particles in air are most likely to cause
explanation.principle: Dust is a particulate pollutant, so its effects are mainly physical and respiratory.
steps: Dust consists of very fine solid particles suspended in air.
When breathed in, such particles can irritate the nose, throat and lungs.
In large quantity, dust also scatters light and reduces visibility.
This is one reason dusty roads and mining areas can be hazardous.
The other options describe unrelated processes.
Thus option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. respiratory irritation and reduced visibility
explanation.simple_explanation: Dust mainly irritates the respiratory system and makes the air hazy. Option A is correct.

question: A family using a charcoal stove in a poorly ventilated room is especially at risk of poisoning by

explanation.question_restate: A family using a charcoal stove in a poorly ventilated room is especially at risk of poisoning by
explanation.principle: Burning charcoal in limited oxygen often produces carbon monoxide, and poor ventilation allows it to accumulate.
steps: Charcoal is mainly carbon.
If carbon burns with enough oxygen, carbon dioxide is formed.
But in a closed or poorly ventilated room, oxygen may become limited.
Then incomplete combustion occurs and carbon monoxide is produced.
Because CO is colourless and odourless, people may not notice it.
It then builds up and can cause serious poisoning by reducing oxygen transport in the blood.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. CO
explanation.simple_explanation: Charcoal in poor ventilation can generate carbon monoxide, a silent poisonous gas. So option B is correct.

question: The gas H₂S may be released during

options.A: decomposition of organic matter containing sulphur

answer_text: decomposition of organic matter containing sulphur

explanation.question_restate: The gas H₂S may be released during
explanation.principle: Hydrogen sulphide often forms in swamps, sewers and decaying protein-rich waste.
steps: Some biological materials contain sulphur in their proteins.
When such matter decays in the absence of enough oxygen, microorganisms can produce H₂S.
This is why H₂S is often linked with drains, sewers, stagnant places and rotting organic matter.
Freezing water, electroplating copper and mixing sand with water do not normally produce H₂S.
Thus option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. decomposition of organic matter containing sulphur
explanation.simple_explanation: H₂S commonly comes from the decay of sulphur-containing organic matter. Option A is correct.

question: Which of the following pollutants is colourless, odourless and highly poisonous?

explanation.question_restate: Which of the following pollutants is colourless, odourless and highly poisonous?
explanation.principle: JAMB often tests recognition of carbon monoxide by its silent but deadly properties.
steps: NO₂ is a brown gas.
SO₂ has a choking smell.
H₂S has a rotten-egg smell.
CO is difficult to detect by human senses because it is colourless and odourless.
Yet it is highly poisonous because it binds strongly with haemoglobin.
Therefore option C is correct.
explanation.final_answer: C. CO
explanation.simple_explanation: CO is the poisonous gas that cannot easily be detected by smell or colour. Option C is correct.

question: One effective way of reducing emission of SO₂ from an industrial chimney is to

options.B: wash the flue gases with alkaline materials
options.D: reduce the height of the chimney

answer_text: wash the flue gases with alkaline materials

explanation.question_restate: One effective way of reducing emission of SO₂ from an industrial chimney is to
explanation.principle: Acidic gases can be removed by reacting them with basic substances.
steps: SO₂ is an acidic oxide.
Acidic oxides react with alkalis or basic materials.
So one common control method is to scrub chimney gases with lime or another alkaline substance.
The alkali neutralizes the acidic gas and reduces its release into the atmosphere.
Burning more sulphur would worsen the problem, while lowering the chimney does not remove the gas.
Hence option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. wash the flue gases with alkaline materials
explanation.simple_explanation: Because SO₂ is acidic, it can be reduced by scrubbing with an alkali. So option B is correct.

question: Acid rain may damage buildings made of limestone mainly because limestone contains

explanation.question_restate: Acid rain may damage buildings made of limestone mainly because limestone contains
explanation.principle: Limestone and marble are attacked by acids because they contain calcium trioxocarbonate(IV), CaCO₃.
steps: Limestone is composed mainly of calcium trioxocarbonate(IV), CaCO₃.
Acid rain contains acidic substances formed from pollutants such as SO₂ and oxides of nitrogen.
Acids react with carbonates.
When acid rain falls on limestone, the carbonate is gradually attacked and the material wears away.
That is why acid rain can damage statues, buildings and monuments made from limestone or marble.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. CaCO₃
explanation.simple_explanation: Limestone contains CaCO₃, which reacts with acids. So option B is correct.

question: The gradual thinning of the ozone layer would most directly lead to an increase in

options.B: amount of dissolved salts in rivers

explanation.question_restate: The gradual thinning of the ozone layer would most directly lead to an increase in
explanation.principle: The protective role of the ozone layer is to absorb much of the Sun's harmful ultraviolet radiation.
steps: The ozone layer lies in the upper atmosphere.
Its important function is to absorb a large part of incoming ultraviolet, UV, radiation.
If the layer is depleted, less UV is absorbed before reaching the Earth's surface.
As a result, exposure to harmful ultraviolet radiation increases.
This can raise risks such as skin damage, eye problems and harm to some living organisms.
The other options are not the direct consequence of ozone depletion.
Therefore option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. ultraviolet radiation reaching the Earth
explanation.simple_explanation: Less ozone means more harmful ultraviolet rays reach the Earth. So option A is correct.

question: An example of a particulate air pollutant is

explanation.question_restate: An example of a particulate air pollutant is
explanation.principle: Particulate pollutants are tiny solid or liquid particles suspended in air.
steps: CO, SO₂ and NO₂ are gaseous pollutants.
Dust is different because it consists of tiny solid particles dispersed in air.
Particles can settle on leaves, soil, roofs and lungs.
The word particulate should make you think of fine matter rather than gases.
Therefore dust is the correct example.
Hence option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. dust
explanation.simple_explanation: Dust is made of tiny particles, not gas molecules, so option B is correct.

question: Which of the following is most likely to increase dust pollution in a town?

options.B: Paving roads and controlling quarry emissions
options.C: Frequent sweeping of dry untarred roads and quarry blasting
options.D: Use of wet scrubbers in industries

answer_text: Frequent sweeping of dry untarred roads and quarry blasting

explanation.question_restate: Which of the following is most likely to increase dust pollution in a town?
explanation.principle: Dust pollution rises when fine particles are easily lifted into the air.
steps: Untarred dry roads release dust easily when disturbed.
Sweeping such roads without moisture can raise more particles into the air.
Quarry blasting also throws fine rock particles into the atmosphere.
Tree planting, paving roads and wet scrubbers help reduce particle emission rather than increase it.
Therefore the activity that most increases dust pollution is option C.
explanation.final_answer: C. Frequent sweeping of dry untarred roads and quarry blasting
explanation.simple_explanation: Dry sweeping and quarry blasting both throw fine particles into the air. So option C is correct.

question: Which pollutant is mainly responsible for reducing the oxygen-carrying capacity of blood?

explanation.question_restate: Which pollutant is mainly responsible for reducing the oxygen-carrying capacity of blood?
explanation.principle: This question focuses on the physiological effect of carbon monoxide.
steps: The body depends on haemoglobin to transport oxygen.
Carbon monoxide binds with haemoglobin strongly and forms a stable complex.
That leaves less haemoglobin available for oxygen transport.
SO₂ mainly irritates the respiratory system and contributes to acid rain.
Dust mainly causes irritation and reduced visibility.
CFCs are chiefly linked with ozone depletion.
Hence CO is the pollutant responsible here.
explanation.final_answer: B. CO
explanation.simple_explanation: CO reduces the blood's ability to carry oxygen. Therefore option B is correct.

question: The pollutant largely produced by volcanic areas, sewers and some petroleum refineries is

explanation.question_restate: The pollutant largely produced by volcanic areas, sewers and some petroleum refineries is
explanation.principle: Hydrogen sulphide is associated with natural and industrial sources that involve sulphur compounds.
steps: H₂S is released in some volcanic regions and in places where sulphur-containing materials decay.
It can also be associated with sewers and some petroleum-processing operations.
Its smell is very characteristic and unpleasant.
Nitrogen, hydrogen and oxygen are not the gases mainly identified with those offensive sulphur-related emissions.
So option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. H₂S
explanation.simple_explanation: Hydrogen sulphide is common in sewers, some refinery areas and volcanic emissions. Option A is correct.

question: Which statement about SO₂ is correct?

options.B: It is an acidic oxide and may irritate the lungs
options.D: It improves the quality of rainwater

answer_text: It is an acidic oxide and may irritate the lungs

explanation.question_restate: Which statement about SO₂ is correct?
explanation.principle: SO₂ behaves chemically as an acidic oxide and also has harmful physiological effects.
steps: SO₂ is formed from sulphur combustion or roasting of sulphide ores.
When dissolved in water it gives an acidic solution, so it is an acidic oxide.
It can irritate the eyes, nose and respiratory system.
It is not basic and it is not a noble gas.
Since it contributes to acid rain, it certainly does not improve rainwater quality.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. It is an acidic oxide and may irritate the lungs
explanation.simple_explanation: SO₂ is an acidic, irritating pollutant, so option B is correct.

question: The use of catalytic converters in motor vehicles helps mainly to reduce

explanation.question_restate: The use of catalytic converters in motor vehicles helps mainly to reduce
explanation.principle: Catalytic converters are pollution-control devices fitted to vehicle exhaust systems.
steps: Vehicle exhaust contains harmful substances such as CO, hydrocarbons and oxides of nitrogen.
A catalytic converter helps transform some of these pollutants into less harmful substances.
This reduces the pollution released into the atmosphere.
It does not treat water hardness or change the nitrogen content of tyres.
Therefore option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. pollutants from exhaust gases
explanation.simple_explanation: Catalytic converters are installed to reduce harmful vehicle exhaust emissions. So option A is correct.

question: Which of the following combinations is most likely in polluted urban air?

explanation.question_restate: Which of the following combinations is most likely in polluted urban air?
explanation.principle: Urban pollution from traffic and industry often involves toxic gases and particulate matter.
steps: Busy cities usually have many vehicles, generators, industrial operations and road dust.
These produce carbon monoxide from incomplete combustion, nitrogen oxides from high-temperature engines and dust particles from roads and construction.
The noble gases and ordinary atmospheric gases listed in the other options are not indicators of pollution.
Therefore the most realistic polluted-air combination is CO, NO₂ and dust.
So option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. CO, NO₂ and dust
explanation.simple_explanation: Urban polluted air commonly contains CO, oxides of nitrogen and dust. Hence option A is correct.

question: An important reason why H₂S is considered dangerous is that it

options.C: is poisonous even at low concentration
options.D: is heavier than all known gases

answer_text: is poisonous even at low concentration

explanation.question_restate: An important reason why H₂S is considered dangerous is that it
explanation.principle: H₂S is not only offensive in smell; it is also toxic.
steps: Many students remember H₂S only for its rotten-egg smell.
But in environmental chemistry, its toxic nature is even more important.
Exposure to H₂S can affect health seriously, especially in confined spaces such as pits or sewers.
It is therefore classified as a dangerous pollutant.
The other statements are not the key correct description required here.
Thus option C is correct.
explanation.final_answer: C. is poisonous even at low concentration
explanation.simple_explanation: H₂S is dangerous because it is poisonous, not just because it smells bad. So option C is correct.

question: Which air pollutant is directly linked with irritation of the respiratory tract and acidification of rainwater?

explanation.question_restate: Which air pollutant is directly linked with irritation of the respiratory tract and acidification of rainwater?
explanation.principle: Sulphur(IV) oxide is a classic example of a respiratory irritant and acid-rain-forming gas.
steps: SO₂ is acidic in nature.
When inhaled, it can irritate the respiratory passages.
When dissolved in atmospheric water, it contributes to acid rain.
Oxygen and nitrogen are normal constituents of air, while hydrogen is not the pollutant described.
Therefore option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. SO₂
explanation.simple_explanation: SO₂ both irritates breathing passages and contributes to acid rain. Option A is correct.

question: Which of the following would most likely reduce CFC pollution?

options.A: greater use of old aerosol propellants containing CFCs
options.D: increase in sulphur content of fuels

explanation.question_restate: Which of the following would most likely reduce CFC pollution?
explanation.principle: To reduce a pollutant, its use must be replaced with safer alternatives.
steps: CFC pollution comes mainly from their use in refrigeration, propellants and foam production.
One practical solution is to replace them with chemicals that do not damage the ozone layer as seriously.
This is why ozone-friendly refrigerants are encouraged.
Using more CFC aerosols would worsen the problem.
Open burning of plastics and increasing sulphur in fuels create other pollution problems.
So option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. use of ozone-friendly refrigerants
explanation.simple_explanation: Replacing CFCs with ozone-friendly alternatives is the right control measure. So option B is correct.

question: The gas mainly responsible for suffocation in a closed garage with a running engine is

explanation.question_restate: The gas mainly responsible for suffocation in a closed garage with a running engine is
explanation.principle: A running engine in an enclosed space is a classic source of carbon monoxide poisoning.
steps: Petrol and diesel engines can produce carbon monoxide, especially when combustion is incomplete.
In an open place the gas disperses, but in a closed garage it can accumulate.
Because CO is colourless and odourless, a person may breathe it in without noticing danger.
It then prevents adequate oxygen transport in the blood, leading to suffocation-like symptoms.
Therefore option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. CO
explanation.simple_explanation: A closed garage with an engine running is a classic carbon monoxide danger. So option A is correct.

question: Which of the following best explains why dust-covered leaves may reduce crop yield?

options.B: Dust may block sunlight and stomata, reducing photosynthesis

answer_text: Dust may block sunlight and stomata, reducing photosynthesis

explanation.question_restate: Which of the following best explains why dust-covered leaves may reduce crop yield?
explanation.principle: Particles settling on leaves can interfere with normal plant processes.
steps: Leaves need light for photosynthesis.
They also exchange gases through tiny pores called stomata.
When dust settles heavily on leaf surfaces, some light is blocked.
The pores may also be partly obstructed.
As a result, photosynthesis and gaseous exchange may reduce, leading to poor plant growth and lower yield.
Hence option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. Dust may block sunlight and stomata, reducing photosynthesis
explanation.simple_explanation: Dust can interfere with leaf function and reduce photosynthesis, so option B is correct.

explanation.principle: Because non-biodegradable waste does not break down easily, it builds up in the environment.
steps: Materials such as plastic, nylon and some cans persist for long periods.
As they accumulate, they make the environment untidy and less useful.
They may block drainage, occupy farmland and interfere with plant growth.
Since they do not act as normal manure, they reduce land quality instead of improving it.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. accumulates and reduces land quality
explanation.simple_explanation: Non-biodegradable waste stays in the soil and builds up, reducing land quality. So option B is correct.

question: Which of the following is most likely to enrich soil with biodegradable waste?

explanation.principle: Biodegradable waste on soil is usually from organic matter.
steps: Food scraps are organic and can decompose under the action of microorganisms.
Ceramic, plastic and glass do not decompose biologically in the same way.
Therefore food scraps are the biodegradable material listed.
Hence option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. Food scraps
explanation.simple_explanation: Food scraps are organic and biodegradable, so option A is correct.

question: Why can oil spillage make farmland less productive?

options.A: It supplies unlimited nitrate to crops
options.B: It may kill useful soil organisms and reduce aeration
options.C: It converts all clay to humus
options.D: It always raises soil temperature to boiling point

answer_text: It may kill useful soil organisms and reduce aeration

explanation.question_restate: Why can oil spillage make farmland less productive?
explanation.principle: Fertile soil depends on living organisms, air spaces and healthy root conditions. Oil harms these.
steps: Useful soil organisms help decomposition and nutrient cycling.
Oil contamination can poison or reduce these organisms.
It can also block pore spaces, thereby reducing aeration of the soil.
Poor aeration and loss of useful microbes reduce healthy root development and soil fertility.
Therefore farmland affected by oil spill becomes less productive.
Option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. It may kill useful soil organisms and reduce aeration
explanation.simple_explanation: Oil spill damages the living and physical condition of soil, so crops grow poorly. Option B is correct.

question: A biodegradable pollutant is preferred to a non-biodegradable one mainly because it

options.B: can be broken down naturally with time

answer_text: can be broken down naturally with time

explanation.question_restate: A biodegradable pollutant is preferred to a non-biodegradable one mainly because it
explanation.principle: The advantage of biodegradable materials is their ability to be decomposed by natural processes.
steps: Biodegradable materials are attacked by bacteria and fungi.
This means they can be converted gradually into simpler substances.
Non-biodegradable materials remain much longer and can accumulate.
Biodegradable does not mean they are never harmful, but they are generally less persistent.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. can be broken down naturally with time
explanation.simple_explanation: Biodegradable materials break down naturally, so option B is correct.

question: Which waste material is least likely to decompose in soil within a short time?

explanation.question_restate: Which waste material is least likely to decompose in soil within a short time?
explanation.principle: Plastic is much more resistant to decomposition than common organic waste.
steps: Fruit peels, paper and cow dung can all eventually be broken down by microorganisms.
Plastic bags persist much longer and are therefore common sources of long-term land pollution.
The question asks for the material least likely to decompose quickly.
That makes option C correct.
explanation.final_answer: C. Plastic bag
explanation.simple_explanation: Plastic bag is the slowest to decay among the options, so option C is correct.

question: One effect of dumping refuse on open land is that it may

options.B: convert all soil nutrients to oxygen
options.D: increase the purity of the environment

explanation.question_restate: One effect of dumping refuse on open land is that it may
explanation.principle: Open dumps often attract flies, rats and other disease-carrying organisms.
steps: Refuse heaps may contain food remnants and moist waste.
These conditions attract flies, cockroaches, rats and other vectors.
Such organisms can spread disease from the dump to homes and food.
Therefore open-land refuse dumping is a health risk.
Option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. encourage breeding of disease vectors
explanation.simple_explanation: Refuse dumps can attract flies and rats that spread disease, so option A is correct.

question: Which statement about non-biodegradable pollutants is correct?

options.A: They are rapidly decomposed by bacteria
options.B: They persist in the environment for long periods
options.C: They are useful fertilizers in all cases

answer_text: They persist in the environment for long periods

explanation.question_restate: Which statement about non-biodegradable pollutants is correct?
explanation.principle: Persistence is the defining danger of many non-biodegradable pollutants.
steps: Because decomposers cannot break them down easily, non-biodegradable pollutants remain for years.
During that time they may accumulate and continue causing environmental problems.
They are not rapidly decomposed, and they are not always useful.
They can pollute both land and water.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. They persist in the environment for long periods
explanation.simple_explanation: Non-biodegradable pollutants stay in the environment for a long time. So option B is correct.

question: Which of the following is the best method of reducing soil pollution by solid waste?

options.A: Throw all waste into nearby bushes
options.B: Proper collection, sorting and disposal of waste
options.C: Burn every type of waste in the open
options.D: Bury plastic waste in shallow pits everywhere

answer_text: Proper collection, sorting and disposal of waste

explanation.question_restate: Which of the following is the best method of reducing soil pollution by solid waste?
explanation.principle: Good waste management is the main control measure for land pollution.
steps: When waste is collected properly, it is less likely to litter the environment.
Sorting allows biodegradable materials, recyclable materials and hazardous materials to be handled appropriately.
Open dumping, open burning and careless burial create additional environmental problems.
Therefore the best answer is proper collection, sorting and disposal.
So option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. Proper collection, sorting and disposal of waste
explanation.simple_explanation: Soil pollution is reduced best by proper waste management. Option B is correct.

question: Oil spilled on soil near a farm is likely to reduce crop yield because it can

options.A: increase root respiration by supplying pure oxygen
options.B: interfere with water uptake and root respiration
options.C: change all soil particles to fertilizer
options.D: remove every microorganism instantly from the entire farm

answer_text: interfere with water uptake and root respiration

explanation.question_restate: Oil spilled on soil near a farm is likely to reduce crop yield because it can
explanation.principle: Roots need both water and oxygen from the soil environment. Oil contamination disturbs both.
steps: Plant roots absorb water from the soil and also require oxygen for respiration.
Oil coating around soil particles can reduce water movement and reduce air spaces.
This makes it harder for roots to take up water and respire normally.
As a result, plant growth slows and yield may fall.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. interfere with water uptake and root respiration
explanation.simple_explanation: Oil-contaminated soil makes it difficult for roots to get enough water and oxygen. So option B is correct.

question: Which of the following is most likely to be converted into humus after some time?

explanation.question_restate: Which of the following is most likely to be converted into humus after some time?
explanation.principle: Humus forms from the decomposition of organic plant and animal remains.
steps: Humus is the dark organic matter formed after decay of dead plant and animal material.
Vegetable peels are organic and can decompose into humus.
Nylon, glass and metal do not form humus.
Therefore option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. Vegetable peels
explanation.simple_explanation: Vegetable peels can decompose into humus, so option A is correct.

question: A major environmental problem caused by non-biodegradable materials in drains and on land is

explanation.question_restate: A major environmental problem caused by non-biodegradable materials in drains and on land is
explanation.principle: Persistent solid waste often causes physical blockage problems.
steps: Plastics and similar waste are easily washed into gutters and drains.
Because they do not decompose quickly, they can remain there and build up.
This blocks water flow and may contribute to flooding.
The other options are unrelated to the direct physical effect of these materials.
Thus option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. blocking of drainage channels
explanation.simple_explanation: Non-biodegradable waste can block drains and cause flooding, so option A is correct.

question: Which of the following wastes would most likely support microbial decomposition in soil?

explanation.question_restate: Which of the following wastes would most likely support microbial decomposition in soil?
explanation.principle: Organic waste is the kind that microbes can feed on and decompose.
steps: Animal dung contains organic substances that microorganisms can use.
Glass, metal and plastic are not readily decomposed biologically.
Hence animal dung is the waste most likely to support microbial decomposition.
Therefore option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. Animal dung
explanation.simple_explanation: Animal dung is organic and easily decomposed by microbes, so option A is correct.

question: Which pair consists of one biodegradable and one non-biodegradable pollutant respectively?

explanation.principle: The order matters: first biodegradable, then non-biodegradable.
steps: Cassava peel is an organic agricultural waste and is biodegradable.
Aluminium can does not decompose biologically in the ordinary sense, so it is non-biodegradable.
Option A is reversed.
Option C is also reversed.

Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. Cassava peel and aluminium can

question: The presence of used batteries carelessly dumped on land is worrying mainly because they may contain

explanation.principle: Some solid wastes are dangerous not only because they persist, but also because of the toxic materials they contain.
steps: Batteries are not ordinary harmless refuse.

If left on land, rainwater can spread some of these chemicals into the environment.

explanation.final_answer: B. harmful chemicals or heavy metals

question: Which of the following is most suitable for compost formation?

steps: Compost is produced when organic waste decomposes under controlled conditions.

Glass, metals, plastics and tyres do not make good compost materials.
Hence option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. Dry leaves and food waste
explanation.simple_explanation: Compost is made from rottable organic matter such as leaves and food waste. So option A is correct.

question: Oil spillage on land may also pollute nearby water bodies when

answer_text: the oil seeps or is washed into streams

explanation.question_restate: Oil spillage on land may also pollute nearby water bodies when
explanation.principle: Pollution often spreads from one part of the environment to another.
steps: Rainfall can wash spilled oil from the soil surface into gutters, streams or ponds.
Some contamination may also move through the ground.
This means a land spill can later become a water-pollution problem.
Therefore option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. the oil seeps or is washed into streams
explanation.simple_explanation: Oil on land can be washed into streams and then pollute water. Option A is correct.

question: Which of the following would least contribute to soil pollution?

explanation.question_restate: Which of the following would least contribute to soil pollution?
explanation.principle: The least contributing factor is the one that actually manages waste properly.
steps: Open dumping, oil spillage and plastic littering all introduce harmful or unsightly materials into the land environment.
Controlled disposal is a management method used to reduce pollution.

Hence option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. Controlled disposal of waste
explanation.simple_explanation: Controlled disposal reduces pollution rather than causing it. So option A is correct.

question: Which statement best explains why non-biodegradable pollutants are a long-term environmental challenge?

options.A: They disappear before they can be noticed
options.B: They accumulate because natural decomposition is very slow or absent

explanation.question_restate: Which statement best explains why non-biodegradable pollutants are a long-term environmental challenge?
explanation.principle: Persistence leads to build-up, and build-up leads to long-term environmental stress.
steps: If a pollutant does not decompose, it remains in the environment.
Each new amount added joins the old amount already present.
This means the total quantity builds up over time.
That persistence is why plastics, cans and similar wastes are serious long-term problems.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. They accumulate because natural decomposition is very slow or absent
explanation.simple_explanation: Non-biodegradable pollutants stay and build up in the environment, making them long-term problems. Option B is correct.

question: Which practice would best reduce soil pollution in a school compound?

options.A: Provide waste bins and separate biodegradable from non-biodegradable waste
options.B: Throw all litter behind the classroom

explanation.principle: Good waste handling starts with proper collection and separation.
steps: Waste bins reduce indiscriminate littering.
Separation allows biodegradable waste to be composted and non-biodegradable waste to be recycled or disposed of properly.
Throwing litter away carelessly or burying plastics randomly still causes pollution.
Therefore option A is the best school-compound practice.
explanation.final_answer: A. Provide waste bins and separate biodegradable from non-biodegradable waste
explanation.simple_explanation: Providing bins and sorting waste is the best way to reduce soil pollution in the compound. So option A is correct.

question: Which of the following materials is most likely to remain in the soil for the longest time?

question: A refuse heap containing food remains is likely to produce bad smell because of

steps: Food remains are biodegradable organic matter.
Microorganisms break them down.
During decomposition, various gases with unpleasant odour may be released.
This explains the bad smell from many refuse heaps.
Therefore option B is correct.

explanation.simple_explanation: Bad smell from food waste comes from microbial decay, so option B is correct.

question: Which set contains only pollutants specifically listed under air pollution in this topic?

explanation.principle: This question checks whether the student can classify the topic items correctly.
steps: The air-pollution list in this topic includes H₂S, CO, SO₂, oxides of nitrogen, chlorofluorocarbons and dust.
Sewage and oil pollution belong to water pollution.
Oil spillage, biodegradable pollutants and non-biodegradable pollutants belong under soil pollution in this topic structure.

Be careful with mixed sets because examiners often combine correct and incorrect categories in one option.
explanation.final_answer: A. H₂S, CO, SO₂ and oxides of nitrogen

question: A town notices the following problems: choking smoke near roads, fish death in a river receiving sewage, and blocked drains filled with plastics. The three problems are respectively examples of

explanation.question_restate: A town notices the following problems: choking smoke near roads, fish death in a river receiving sewage, and blocked drains filled with plastics. The three problems are respectively examples of
explanation.principle: The question combines three environments and asks for the correct order.
steps: Choking smoke near roads is an air-pollution problem because it affects the atmosphere and breathing.
Fish death in a river receiving sewage is a water-pollution problem.
Blocked drains filled with plastics result mainly from land or soil pollution by non-biodegradable solids.
So the correct order is air, water and soil pollution.
Therefore option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. air, water and soil pollution
explanation.simple_explanation: Smoke is air pollution, sewage in rivers is water pollution, and plastics on land are soil pollution. So option A is correct.

question: Which of the following control measures is correctly matched with the pollutant?

options.C: oil spill — stir vigorously to spread it faster
options.D: plastic waste — throw into drainage channels

answer_text: CO — use of proper ventilation and efficient combustion

explanation.question_restate: Which of the following control measures is correctly matched with the pollutant?
explanation.principle: Pollution control questions test whether the student knows a sensible preventive measure for each pollutant.
steps: CO is produced mainly by incomplete combustion, so efficient burning and good ventilation help reduce danger.
Sewage should not be dumped into wells because that contaminates drinking water.
Oil spill should be contained, not spread.
Plastic waste should not be put into drains because it may block them.
Therefore the only correctly matched pair is option A.
explanation.final_answer: A. CO — use of proper ventilation and efficient combustion
explanation.simple_explanation: Good ventilation and efficient combustion help prevent CO poisoning, so option A is correct.

question: Which statement best summarizes the difference between biodegradable and non-biodegradable pollutants?

options.A: Biodegradable pollutants are always gases, non-biodegradable pollutants are always solids
options.B: Biodegradable pollutants can be broken down by living organisms, while non-biodegradable pollutants persist for much longer
options.C: Biodegradable pollutants are harmless, non-biodegradable pollutants are always poisonous
options.D: Biodegradable pollutants occur only in soil, non-biodegradable pollutants occur only in water

answer_text: Biodegradable pollutants can be broken down by living organisms, while non-biodegradable pollutants persist for much longer

explanation.question_restate: Which statement best summarizes the difference between biodegradable and non-biodegradable pollutants?
explanation.principle: The most important contrast is decomposability and persistence.
steps: Biodegradable pollutants are materials that bacteria and fungi can act on and break down.
Non-biodegradable pollutants resist this natural decomposition.
As a result, non-biodegradable substances stay in the environment for a longer time and accumulate.
It is not true that all biodegradable materials are harmless, nor that all non-biodegradable ones are poisonous.
The key difference is natural breakdown versus persistence.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. Biodegradable pollutants can be broken down by living organisms, while non-biodegradable pollutants persist for much longer
explanation.simple_explanation: Biodegradable means capable of natural biological breakdown; non-biodegradable means persistent. So option B is correct.

question: A student says: 'Dust is not a pollutant because it is just soil in the air.' The best response is that dust becomes a pollutant when it

options.B: is suspended in air in harmful amount and affects health or visibility

answer_text: is suspended in air in harmful amount and affects health or visibility

explanation.question_restate: A student says: 'Dust is not a pollutant because it is just soil in the air.' The best response is that dust becomes a pollutant when it
explanation.principle: Many substances become pollutants because of their amount and harmful effect, not simply because of what they are made of.
steps: Dust may consist largely of fine soil or other solid particles.
That alone does not answer whether it is a pollutant.
Once it is present in air in enough quantity to reduce visibility, irritate breathing or settle harmfully on plants and property, it is acting as a pollutant.
This fits the general definition of pollution: harmful presence in the environment.
Therefore the best correction to the student's statement is option B.
explanation.final_answer: B. is suspended in air in harmful amount and affects health or visibility
explanation.simple_explanation: Dust is a pollutant when enough of it is in the air to cause harm, especially to breathing and visibility. So option B is correct.
